description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Central in AOS-CX 10.13.1150?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for SNMP in AOS-CX 10.13.1150?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Port Access (802.1x, MAC Authentication, Device Profile), Port Security, IPv4/v6 Source Lockdown, Dynamic ARP Inspection and/or DHCPv4/v6 Snooping configurations are mutually exclusive with PSM stateful firewall policies.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Port Access?

Feature Caveat: Port Access (802.1x, MAC Authentication, Device Profile), Port Security, IPv4/v6 Source Lockdown, Dynamic ARP Inspection and/or DHCPv4/v6 Snooping configurations are mutually exclusive with PSM stateful firewall policies.

description: BFD sessions are not supported on sub interfaces. Use a switch virtual interfaces (SVI) to configure a BFD session.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Subinterfaces?
Feature Caveat: Subinterfaces - BFD sessions are not supported on sub interfaces. Use a switch virtual interface (SVI) to configure a BFD session.

description: Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for REST in AOS-CX 10.13.1150?
Feature Caveat: REST - Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

description: For locally-switched and routed flows on the switch, the traffic from the host is subject to policy processing only once and only egress policy is enforced on the traffic egressing the workload and entering the switch.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Stateful L4 firewall in AOS-CX 10.13.1150?

Feature Caveat: Stateful L4 firewall - For locally-switched and routed flows on the switch, the traffic from the host is subject to policy processing only once and only egress policy is enforced on the traffic egressing the workload and entering the switch.

description: Stateful services for VRFs, where route leaking is enabled, are not supported.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what limitation is mentioned for Stateful L4 firewall in AOS-CX 10.13.1150?
Stateful services for VRFs, where route leaking is enabled, are not supported.

description: Port-access (802.1x, MAC authentication, Device Profile), Port-security, DHCP v4/v6 snooping, Dynamic ARP Inspection and/or IPv4/v6 Source Lockdown configurations are mutually exclusive with PSM Stateful firewall policies

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what additional caveat is documented for Stateful L4 firewall?

Feature Caveat: Stateful L4 firewall - Port-access (802.1x, MAC authentication, Device Profile), Port-security, DHCP v4/v6 snooping, Dynamic ARP Inspection and/or IPv4/v6 Source Lockdown configurations are mutually exclusive with PSM Stateful firewall policies.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1.

Edge (Windows) requires a minimum supported version of 41.

Chrome (Ubuntu) requires a minimum supported version of 76 (desktop).

Firefox (Ubuntu) requires a minimum supported version of 56.

Compatibility: Firefox (Ubuntu) requires minimum supported version 56.

Safari (MacOS) requires a minimum supported version of 12.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, which version of Safari (MacOS) is supported?
Compatibility: Safari (MacOS) requires minimum supported version 12.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what is the minimum supported version for Safari (iOS)?
Compatibility: Safari (iOS) requires a minimum supported version of 10 (Version 12 is not supported).
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, which version of Safari (iOS) is supported?
Compatibility: Safari (iOS) requires a minimum supported version of 10. Version 12 is not supported.

description: Symptom: Notification clients stop receiving updates, sessions drop, a core dump is recorded, and the REST service restarts. Scenario: This issue may occur when a subscription is added or removed at the same exact time notifications are being delivered. Workaround: Wait for the REST service to complete its automatic restart, then reconnect the notification clients and reapply the desired subscriptions.

description: Symptom: Insufficient argument validation could cause a system crash when executing certain ovs-appctl mirror commands with incomplete parameters. Scenario: Executing mirror management commands with an improper number of arguments could cause memory access violations, potentially resulting in a switch crash. Workaround: Commands now verify that the required number of arguments is provided before processing operations. When insufficient arguments are detected, error messages are displayed with usage guidance.
symptom: Insufficient argument validation could cause a system crash when executing certain ovs-appctl mirror commands with incomplete parameters.

description: Symptom: Container statuses are not updated after deleting and recreating the containers. Scenario: This issue can occur with these following time sensitive steps: 1. Container A is created, Container Manager is processing the creation of said container. 2. A delete request for container A is sent using CLI or REST. 3. A request to create container A is sent. 4. When checking for statuses using REST or show container, the container is marked as down. Workaround: Where possible, avoid deleting and recreating the same container while it is being created, allow it to reach the operational state. If the issue is encountered, delete the container using REST or CLI and restart the switch to release the occupied container resources.

description: Symptom: STP ports are entering into Disabled/Blocking state. Scenario: This issue occurs after an edge device log-off and log-in back to a secured port or when a server connected to VSX pair is rebooted. Workaround: Reboot the PVST process.

section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, DL.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.
description: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, DL.10.xx.yyyy). Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade on AOS-CX 10.13.1150?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, DL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, DL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
description: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, for 10,000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150 devices, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the 'boot system <BOOT-BANK>' command and enter 'n' when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, ensure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the 'allow unsafe updates' command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the 'boot system <BOOT-BANK>' command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, for 10,000 AOS-CX 10.13.1150 devices, what are the steps to perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering 'n' when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

description: Symptom: Container statuses are not updated after deleting and recreating the containers. Scenario: This issue can occur with these following time sensitive steps: 1. Container A is created, Container Manager is processing the creation of said container. 2. A delete request for container A is sent using CLI or REST. 3. A request to create container A is sent. 4. When checking for statuses using REST or show container, the container is marked as down. Workaround: Where possible, avoid deleting and recreating the same container while it is being created, allow it to reach the operational state. If the issue is encountered, delete the container using REST or CLI and restart the switch to release the occupied container resources.
